samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Pulse detection module. Detects rising edges of data_in and outputs a pulse.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state name="INITIAL">Initial state.</state>
        <state name="FIRST_ONE">First '1' detected.</state>
        <state name="SECOND_ZERO">Second '0' detected after first '1'.</state>
        <state name="PULSE_END">End of pulse detected.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <register id="state" width_description="[2:0]" type="reg">State register.</register>
        <wire id="next_state" width_description="[2:0]">Next state wire.</wire>
        
        <always_comb>
            case(state)
                INITIAL: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = FIRST_ONE;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = INITIAL;
                    end
                end
                
                FIRST_ONE: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = SECOND_ZERO;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = FIRST_ONE;
                    end
                end
                
                SECOND_ZERO: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = PULSE_END;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = SECOND_ZERO;
                    end
                end
                
                PULSE_END: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = INITIAL;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = FIRST_ONE;
                    end
                end
                
                default: next_state = INITIAL;
            endcase
        </always_comb>
        
        <always_ff posedge clk negedge rst_n> 
            if(~rst_n) begin
                state <= INITIAL;
            end else begin
                state <= next_state;
            end
        </always_ff>
        
        <assign data_out = (state == PULSE_END && data_in == 1'b0) ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;>
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>Use a state machine to detect pulses based on the input signal.</implementation>
</module>
